free_text: Analyzing power and cross section distributions of
the 12C(p,pa)8Be cluster knockout reaction at an
incident energy of 100 MeV
Experiment performed at the
separated-sector cyclotron facility of iThemba
Laboratory for Accelerator Based Sciences
Self-supporting carbon foil of 2.2 mg/cm2 thickness
inside a 1.5 m diameter scattering chamber
The telescope for proton
detection consisted of a 1 mm thick Si
surface-barrier transmission detector followed by a
7.6 cm diameter by 5.1 cm thick NaI(Tl) scintillator
Telescope for alpha particle
detection consisted of a 100 mu-m thick
surface-barrier transmission detector followed by 2
mm thick surface barrier transmission detectors.
Behind this telescope was a 7.6 cm diameter by 5.1
cm thick NaI(Tl) crystal-phototube assembly, which
acted as a veto for energetic protons.
E-dE method was used to identify charged particles
Alpha-proton coincidences were required
A 101+-0.5MeV proton beam, polarized to
approximately 80%
Statistical uncertainty
Compiled by S.H.
Data presented in fig.2 sent by author (J.M.)
